methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Krevera is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Edward Schneeweiss that has raised $3M+. The sector peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparator (277810.KQ), including relevant private firms such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Valuation was derived using the Stage-Progression method, which proved applicable while peer-median comparison was not. The Stage-Progression approach uses the $3M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement in the sector, yielding an implied valuation of $8M. This method alone anchors the analysis, as the narrow peer set within Robotics & Manufacturing—while 11 peers exist in the broader sector—failed to yield three or more datapoints at equivalent stage or valuation tier required for robust peer-median triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M (low) to $9M (high), with a median of $8M. This range reflects ±15% bounds around the stage-progression median. Key uncertainties include the absence of same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable valuations, which limits cross-validation of the stage-multiplier assumption. Additional data on recent Seed-stage financings or public market multiples for robotics peers would improve confidence in this range. The reliance on a single method warrants treating this as a directional estimate pending additional comparable evidence.
